one_time_description: Williams Treaty distribution to members recorded as expense in Land Management segment
economic_notes: Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership gaming revenue received. Williams Treaty settlement funds held as restricted surplus ($10,000,000 externally restricted, plus interest and distribution reserves). Subdivision construction underway financed by BMO construction line of credit. Islands in the Trent trust fund for youth per-capita distributions.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Large deficit driven by $5,734,451 Williams Treaty distribution to members in Land Management segment. Significant restricted cash/investments related to Williams Treaty settlement (~$57M combined in cash and investments). Trust Fund (Islands in the Trent Youth Allocation Fund, $3.5M) excluded from main financial statements. No schedule of remuneration disclosed. Accounts receivable calculated as Due from Government ($1,322,864) + Other accounts receivable ($413,021) = $1,735,885. Current liabilities calculated as construction line of credit ($1,595,110) + accounts payable ($7,344,283) + due to government ($1,342,357) = $10,281,750. Prior year accumulated surplus taken from beginning of 2021 year ($98,659,552) which equals end of 2020.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
